next_steps: Orbital tissue biopsy for histopathology, immunohistochemistry for IgG4, and IgG4/IgG plasma cell ratio
Serum soluble interleukin-2 receptor and chest imaging (CT chest/PET) to evaluate for sarcoidosis
Consider a trial of systemic corticosteroids to assess for rapid clinical improvement of orbital inflammation
Evaluate for underlying rheumatologic conditions (e.g., ANA, ENA panel, anti-dsDNA)
Perform orbital tissue biopsy with immunohistochemistry for IgG4, IgG, and IgG4/IgG ratio
Repeat skin biopsy with direct immunofluorescence to evaluate for lupus erythematosus
Repeat ANA and ENA (anti-Ro/SSA, anti-La/SSB) panel
Obtain PET/CT scan to assess for systemic hypermetabolic sites for potential biopsy
Ocular surface assessment including Schirmer test
Consider a therapeutic trial of systemic corticosteroids after diagnostic biopsy samples are obtained
